title: Customer conflict back-to-back false positive — booking trapped in loop after 'different time'
failing_behavior: Customer has existing appointment at 3:15 PM (ends 4:15 PM). After conflict detection, user chooses 'different time' and AI correctly proposes 4:15 PM (next slot after existing ends). System re-fires customer_conflict because the existing appointment ends exactly at 4:15 PM (aEnd==reqStart). With >= boundary the check evaluates as true → conflict message shown again. User is permanently trapped: every new time proposed at exactly 4:15 PM causes the same false-positive loop.
root_cause: NailAvailabilityChecker customer conflict overlap check (line 586) used an inline condition `aStart < reqEndMins && (aStart + aDur) >= reqStartMins` instead of the shared `_overlaps()` helper. `_overlaps()` uses strict `>` on the right boundary (aEnd > reqStart), allowing back-to-back appointments (aEnd === reqStart). The inline `>=` caused back-to-back to be falsely flagged. The staff conflict check correctly uses `_overlaps()`. The customer conflict check did not — inconsistency introduced the regression.
fix_summary: Replaced inline `>= reqStartMins` overlap condition in the customer conflict check with `_overlaps(reqStartMins, reqEndMins, aStart, aStart + aDur)`. This is the same helper used by the staff conflict check, `_findAlternativeSlots`, and the free-staff finder. Back-to-back (aEnd === reqStart) is now correctly allowed for customers. Genuine overlaps (aEnd > reqStart) are still caught. Comment updated to explain why _overlaps() is the right choice.

conversation.content: I like to book a pedicure with John today at 3PM. My phone is 4084397522 and I am JD

John specializes in gel services only. Helen, Tracy, or Loan can do your pedicure — which would you prefer?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Pedicure"],"staff":null,"date":"2026-04-09","time":"15:00","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]

You already have Eyelash Extensions with Loan Nguyen at 15:15 that day. Would you like to replace it, keep it, or pick a different time?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":"15:00","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]

Of course! What time would you like instead?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":null,"name":"JD","phone":"4084397522","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]
put me in the next one after my appointment with Loan

Your appointment with Loan is at 3:15 PM. A pedicure takes 60 minutes, so the next slot would be around 4:15 PM. Shall I book your pedicure with Helen at 4:15 PM today?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":"16:15","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522","lang":"en","pendingAction":"booking_offer","existingBookingId":null}]

Your Pedicure with Helen at 4:15 PM is confirmed and booked. We look forward to seeing you! Feel free to reach out if you need to make any changes or book anything else.
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":"16:15","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]
[BOOKING:{"services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":"16:15","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522"}]
[ESCALATE:appointment]

conflict_resolution_behavior.turn_3_original_conflict: 3:00 PM for Helen overlaps existing Loan appointment at 3:15 PM → correctly flagged
conflict_resolution_behavior.turn_5_proposal_4_15: 4:15 PM early check: existing ends exactly at 4:15 PM → back-to-back → with _overlaps (strict >) → valid → not flagged
conflict_resolution_behavior.turn_6_confirmation: ESCALATE fires with time=16:15 → availability check at 16:15 → _overlaps returns false for aEnd=975, reqStart=975 → valid → booking proceeds

notes: _overlaps() is defined at line 213: `return aStart < bEnd && aEnd > bStart`. Strict > on right boundary = back-to-back allowed. The staff conflict check has always used _overlaps() correctly. The customer conflict check had a divergent inline condition with >= that was documented as intentional but was in fact wrong — it prevented customers from booking the first available slot immediately after an existing appointment ends.
confidence_gap: Runner verifies: _overlaps call present in customer-conflict section. Live testing required to confirm that the 4:15 PM booking completes correctly in production with real Firestore data (existing appointment duration must be confirmed as 60 min in the real appointment record).
